note: Flow Designer flows are visual and stored as internal flow/action records, not flat JS. This file is a build blueprint: recreate these steps in Flow Designer exactly as listed.

steps.details: Map catalog variables to table fields as defined in catalog_items/onboarding_catalog_item.json. Set state = New.
Alternatively, rely purely on the AutoAssignTasks business rule fired by the record insert in Step 1 — recommended to keep logic in one place (business rule) rather than duplicating in the flow.
Wait until x_onb_lifecycle_request.state == 'Tasks Assigned' (confirms business rule completed).
Send 'Onboarding Request Created' notification to HR Operations group and the manager (see notifications/notification_definitions.json).
Wait until x_onb_lifecycle_request.state == 'Closed Complete' (set by UpdateRequestStatus business rule when all 3 dept tasks close).
Send 'Onboarding Complete' notification to HR Operations and the manager, confirming employee is fully provisioned.
Close the originating catalog request item (RITM) as Closed Complete, linking back to the fulfilled x_onb_lifecycle_request.
